summary: This cluster investigated the taxonomic utility of chloroplast lobe morphology within the red algal genus *Laurencia* and its close relative *Yuzurua*. Researchers employed advanced microscopy techniques, including confocal laser scanning microscopy (LSM), transmission electron microscopy (TEM), and high-resolution scanning electron microscopy (HRSEM), to compare species. The key finding was that *Yuzurua poiteaui* var. *gemmifera* possessed significantly larger chloroplast lobes than *Laurencia dendroidea*, establishing lobe size, shape, and distribution as a reliable taxonomic characteristic. The geographic and ecological scope of this work was not specified in the provided summaries.
